title: chlora sessilis
intro: Chlora sessilis, also known as the sessile buttonbush, is a shrub plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, thanks to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Chlora sessilis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has also been used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and salves, to support skin health and relieve pain.
